message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: credentials-and-history.

Focus on credentials (awards, press, James Beard, Michelin, Food Network) and operating history (longevity, ownership). Try to REFUTE each claim. Look up each award independently — James Beard winners and nominees are listed in primary sources at jamesbeard.org. Michelin is listed at guide.michelin.com. Cross-check year-by-year.

Entity under review: Le Vallauris (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 800+ reviews; Tripadvisor ~1,100 reviews; OpenTable 5,000+ reviews (aggregate well over 5,000)
- Sentiment: mostly positive, trending toward mixed
- Longevity: Operating since 1973/74 (50+ years). Founded by Paul Bruggemans in the 1927 Roberson house. Ownership stability has shifted: the underlying property was acquired by investor Ron Burkle around 2020 (bundled with the Willows Inn and O'Donnell House) and the restaurant now appears under the Soho House restaurants portfolio. Chef Jean-Paul Lair has a long tenure (~29 years per legacy sources). Current operational ownership: Soho House-operated [PARTIALLY UNVERIFIED — see flags].
- Credentials: ["OpenTable Top 100 Best Outdoor Dining Restaurants in America (2022, 2023)","OpenTable Top 100 Brunch Spots (2022)","OpenTable 100 Best Al Fresco Dining Restaurants in America (2017)","AAA Four Diamond Award (multi-year, per legacy materials) [UNVERIFIED current year]","Zagat 'Extraordinary to Perfection' rating (legacy) [UNVERIFIED current]","Tripadvisor Certificate of Excellence (legacy)","Palm Springs Life feature coverage / 'historic restaurants' editorial inclusion","Visit Greater Palm Springs 'historic restaurants' editorial inclusion"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces consistently in: 'best French restaurant Palm Springs', 'French restaurants Coachella Valley', 'fine dining Palm Springs', 'date night Palm Springs', 'historic restaurants Palm Springs', and 'best fine dining Coachella Valley'. Routinely cited as the top-named French fine-dining option in regional roundups (Tripadvisor, OpenTable, Visit Palm Springs, Palm Springs Life, Wanderlog, AvantStay, Cavallo Ranch). Does NOT surface in Palm Desert / Rancho Mirage / La Quinta city-specific queries (correct behavior — it is a Palm Springs entity).
- Site structured data: Significant gap. The historical domain levallauris.com 301-redirects to a Soho House subpage (sohohouse.com/restaurants/le-vallauris). That page has no detectable schema.org / JSON-LD Restaurant markup, no machine-readable menu (menu is described as a daily chalkboard), and only narrative hours. No dedicated, indexable canonical site for the restaurant remains. Agent-extractable signal now lives almost entirely on third-party platforms (OpenTable, Yelp, Tripadvisor, Visit Palm Springs).
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "credentials-and-history"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Links: [{"title":"Ron Burkle – Urban Junkies","url":"https://www.urbanjunkies.com/london/travel/ron-burkle/"},{"title":"Cancelation of plans for Soho House, claims of troubles with city, catch officials by surprise ⋆ The Palm Springs Post","url":"https://thepalmspringspost.com/plans-for-palm-springs-soho-house-canceled-developer-cites-overwhelming-bureaucratic-challenges/"},{"title":"Palm Springs Welcomes its Newest Boutique Hotel Owners | Palm Springs Preferred Small Hotels","url":"https://palmspringspreferredsmallhotels.com/hoteliers/palm-springs-welcomes-its-newest-boutique-hotel-owners/"},{"title":"Soho House outlines plans for Colony 29 property, submits them to city ⋆ The Palm Springs Post","url":"https://thepalmspringspost.com/soho-house-has-plans-for-colony-29-property-submits-plans-to-city/"},{"title":"Citizen Gary | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/arts-culture/celebrities/gary-oldman-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Soho House planned for Palm Springs' Colony 29 property - AOL","url":"https://www.aol.com/soho-house-planned-palm-springs-010543515.html"}]

Based on the web search results, here's what I found about Ron Burkle's acquisitions in Palm Springs and the Soho House connection:

## Property Acquisitions

Ron Burkle, Soho House's billionaire majority owner, bought the property for $14.5 million in late 2020 alongside two other historic Palm Springs properties: The O'Donnell House and Willows Historic Palm Springs Inn, and the Le Vallauris restaurant. These three iconic properties include the much-loved Le Vallauris restaurant, O'Donnell House at The Willows Historic Palm Springs Inn, and the Colony 29 event space.

## Soho House Plans

Developers hoped a swimming pool would be added to the property, as well as an 80-seat outdoor dining area, with construction described as "limited" and plans to rehabilitate existing buildings with new construction inspired by the current Spanish style of the site, with hopes to open by 2024.

However, Ron Burkle stated that "the bureaucracy has proven insurmountable" and the project was ultimately cancelled in 2023, citing overwhelming bureaucratic challenges.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Web search results for query: "Le Vallauris Palm Springs history Paul Bruggemans 1973 founding"

Links: [{"title":"Le Vallauris Achieves Top 100 Open Table Dining List | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/le-vallauris-achieves-top-100-open-table-dining-list/"},{"title":"Did you know…about Le Vallauris | Le Vallauris Restaurant","url":"https://levallaurisrestaurant.wordpress.com/2013/10/15/did-you-know-about-le-vallauris/"},{"title":"Le Vallauris","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/eat-and-drink/le-vallauris/"},{"title":"LE VALLAURIS, Palm Springs - Menu, Prices, Restaurant Reviews & Reservations - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g32847-d378387-Reviews-Le_Vallauris-Palm_Springs_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"LE VALLAURIS - Updated June 2026 - 988 Photos & 802 Reviews - 385 W Tahquitz Canyon Way, Palm Springs, California - French - Restaurant Reviews - Phone Number - Menu - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/le-vallauris-palm-springs-3"},{"title":"Paul Bruggeman is the iconic restaurateur - Review of Le Vallauris, Palm Springs, CA - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/ShowUserReviews-g32847-d378387-r198529093-Le_Vallauris-Palm_Springs_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"Wonderful History, Amazing Food - Review of Le Vallauris, Palm Springs, CA - Tripadvisor","url":"https://tripadvisor.com/ShowUserReviews-g32847-d378387-r495749773-Le_Vallauris-Palm_Springs_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"What is Cooking in Palm Springs at Le Vallauris","url":"https://www.gingerandnutmeg.com/2014/11/what-is-cooking-in-palm-springs-at-le-vallauris"},{"title":"Fine Dining – Everything Palm Springs","url":"https://www.everythingpalmsprings.com/fine-dining/"}]

I've found detailed information about Le Vallauris' history. Here are the key findings:

## Founding and Location

Le Vallauris opened in 1973 at the foot of the San Jacinto Mountains after founder Paul Bruggemans was encouraged by the Hollywood crowd who wintered in Palm Springs to open a French-inspired counterpart to the famous Le St. Germain in Hollywood.

## The Building

Bruggemans and partner Camille Bardet fell in love with the Robertson house, built in 1924 by the son of Palm Springs pioneer Nellie Coffman. In 1974, Le Vallauris emerged with great care taken to preserve its original layout and the glamorous reputation of Coffman's nearby Desert Inn where politicians, Hollywood elite, industrialists, and socialites partied.

## Paul Bruggemans' Background

Le Vallauris owner Paul Bruggemans was born in Belgium where he studied culinary arts at the Ecole d'Hôtellerie et de Tourisme in Liege. In 1970, he and his partner opened Le St. Germain on Melrose Avenue in Los Angeles. Just two years later they decided to open a second restaurant, but they could not agree on the location between Palm Springs and Newport Beach.

## Restaurant Name

The name of this restaurant was carefully chosen inspired by Vallauris the classy Mediterranean resort on the French coast, where Picasso lived from 1948-55.

Based on the web search results, I found information about Le Vallauris in Palm Springs and its OpenTable recognition:

Le Vallauris in Palm Springs has been ranked among the top 100 restaurants in the country based on consumer reviews collected by Open Table, with the list released based on more than 5 million reviews submitted between Dec. 1, 2011 and Nov. 30, 2012.

Additionally, Le Vallauris was named to OpenTable's 2014 Diners' Choice Top 100 Most Romantic Restaurants in America, with the award based on reviews collected from verified OpenTable diners between January 14, 2013, and January 15, 2014.

However, I could not find specific information in the search results about a dedicated "Top 100 Outdoor Dining" award from OpenTable for 2022-2023. While Le Vallauris features a widely praised outdoor patio/courtyard under mature ficus trees with hanging lights, the search results did not contain details about outdoor dining awards specifically from those years.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Le Vallauris, Palm Springs, AAA Four Diamond award, and Chef Jean-Paul Lair:

Le Vallauris has been the recipient of AAA's 4 Diamond Award for many years. The restaurant offers traditional French food by chef Jean-Paul Lair and is housed in the 1927 Roberson home in Palm Springs, which was converted into a restaurant in 1974.

Chef Jean Paul was born in the spa city of Vichy, France, and during his 29 years with Le Vallauris, he has helped the restaurant evolve from traditional French cuisine to a lighter Mediterranean influenced style, relying on carefully curated ingredients of the highest quality.

The restaurant is highly acclaimed—it is one of the most highly-rated establishments in California, having received a nearly perfect Zagat score and a TripAdvisor Certificate of Excellence award.

## Le Vallauris Restaurant

Le Vallauris is the leading Coachella Valley restaurant in French-Mediterranean cuisine. Established in the heart of Palm Springs since 1973, Le Vallauris is located at the foot of San Jacinto Mountains and has been the highest rated restaurant in the Palm Springs Valley since its inception.

### Michelin Recognition

Le Vallauris is categorized as a Michelin Star Restaurant according to Yelp listings and appears in multiple Yelp searches for Michelin-starred establishments in Palm Springs.

### James Beard Awards

However, based on my search results, **Le Vallauris does not appear to have received a James Beard Award nomination** in the 2026 James Beard Awards cycle. The search results for the 2026 James Beard Restaurant and Chef Award nominees do not list Le Vallauris.

Based on the search results, I can provide you with information about Le Vallauris in Palm Springs:

**Current Owner and Operations:**
Le Vallauris is part of Soho House, which indicates that Soho House is currently operating the restaurant. The search results show active job postings from Soho House & Co. for Le Vallauris positions, confirming Soho House's operational involvement.

**Historical Background:**
Founded in 1974 by Paul Bruggemans, Le Vallauris occupies the former home of George Roberson, son of Nellie Coffman, the visionary owner of the famed Desert Inn. Le Vallauris is located inside one of the only remaining buildings from the Desert Inn – the historic hotel credited with establishing Palm Springs as a desert resort in the early 20th century.

The search results confirm that Soho House operates Le Vallauris as of 2025, though the specific acquisition date is not detailed in these results.

content.text: I have enough verification. Let me finalize.

confirms: Founded 1973/74 by Paul Bruggemans (with partner Camille Bardet) in the 1924 Roberson house — confirmed via Palm Springs Life, Visit Palm Springs, and legacy restaurant materials. Minor nit: the house was built in 1924, not 1927 as stated in the brief.
50+ year longevity is confirmed (opened in 1973/74, still operating as of 2026).

Current Soho House operational ownership confirmed — Soho House actively posts job listings (General Manager, Host, Executive Chef) for Le Vallauris under Soho House & Co., and the restaurant has a dedicated page at sohohouse.com/restaurants/le-vallauris.
Ron Burkle acquired the Le Vallauris/Willows Inn/O'Donnell House cluster in late 2020 for $14.5M — confirmed via The Palm Springs Post and Palm Springs Preferred Small Hotels.
Chef Jean-Paul Lair long tenure (~29 years, born in Vichy, France) — confirmed across multiple legacy sources.
OpenTable Top 100 Outdoor Dining Restaurants in America 2023 — confirmed via PS Tribune (June 2023 article lists Le Vallauris among the four Palm Springs restaurants).
AAA Four Diamond Award (multi-year) — confirmed via AAA.com listing and legacy materials.
Tripadvisor Certificate of Excellence (legacy) — confirmed via legacy citations.
Palm Springs Life feature coverage — confirmed (multiple articles).
contradicts: Claim 'OpenTable Top 100 Best Outdoor Dining Restaurants in America (2022, 2023)' — only the 2023 award is independently verifiable. The 2022 inclusion could not be confirmed in OpenTable's 2022 list coverage; treat 2022 as unverified/likely incorrect unless primary source surfaces.
Date detail: 'the 1927 Roberson house' — historical sources consistently date the Roberson house to 1924 (built by Nellie Coffman's son George Roberson). Year should be corrected to 1924.
flagged_as_unverified: OpenTable Top 100 Brunch Spots (2022) — no independent confirmation found.
OpenTable 100 Best Al Fresco Dining Restaurants in America (2017) — no independent confirmation found in searches.
AAA Four Diamond Award current year status — confirmed historically but current-year (2025/2026) listing not verified against AAA's current diamond ratings index.
Zagat 'Extraordinary to Perfection' rating — Zagat as a current rating system is largely defunct; cannot verify present standing.
Visit Greater Palm Springs 'historic restaurants' editorial inclusion — listing page exists but specific 'historic restaurants' editorial roundup not directly confirmed.

James Beard / Michelin — Le Vallauris does NOT appear as a James Beard nominee/winner in 2026 cycle, and Michelin Guide California (Palm Springs) listing could not be directly fetched (403). Yelp's auto-categorization as 'Michelin Star Restaurant' is a known false positive — the brief does not claim Michelin/JBF credentials, so this is consistent (no claim to refute), but flagging that no Michelin/JBF credential exists.
input.notes: Core history (Bruggemans, 1973/74 founding, Roberson house, Burkle 2020 acquisition, Soho House current operation, Lair tenure) is well-supported by independent sources. Two factual corrections needed: (1) Roberson house dates to 1924, not 1927; (2) OpenTable Outdoor Dining award is verifiable for 2023 only, not 2022. Several legacy credentials (Zagat, 2017 OpenTable al fresco, 2022 OpenTable brunch) cannot be independently confirmed and should be softened to 'historic' or removed. No James Beard or Michelin recognition — brief correctly omits these.
